summary: Web-research + spec the References tab features

specs.feature: References tab → Table of Contents group: the TOC gallery dropdown (Automatic Table 1, Automatic Table 2, Manual Table, Custom Table of Contents…, Remove Table of Contents, Save Selection to Table of Contents Gallery…), the Add Text dropdown (Do Not Show in Table of Contents, Level 1/2/3), and the Update Table button. The TOC is generated from the document's h1/h2/h3 headings, with right-aligned page numbers (computed via WC.Editor.pageOfElement) joined by a dotted tab leader, each entry a clickable link that jumps to its heading.
specs.wordBehavior:
Exact Word behavior confirmed via web research.

TABLE OF CONTENTS DROPDOWN GALLERY (References → Table of Contents button), in order:
- "Built-in" header, then:
  - Automatic Table 1 — title text "Contents", auto-generated from Heading 1–3, dotted tab leader, right-aligned page numbers.
  - Automatic Table 2 — IDENTICAL except the title reads "Table of Contents". The ONLY difference between Automatic 1 and 2 is that title word ("Contents" vs "Table of Contents"). Confirmed by CustomGuide and nutsandbolts.
  - Manual Table — title "Table of Contents"; inserts editable placeholder rows the user types over; Word does NOT update it from headings. Well-known placeholder strings: "Type chapter title (level 1)" / "(level 2)" / "(level 3)" with placeholder page numbers "1/2/3"; level 2/3 lines indented.
- "More Tables from Office.com" (online gallery — document as cloud/not-implemented).
- "Custom Table of Contents…" — opens the Table of Contents dialog (below).
- "Remove Table of Contents" — deletes the TOC field.
- "Save Selection to Table of Contents Gallery…" — saves selection as a building block (not-implemented; no building-block store).

CUSTOM TABLE OF CONTENTS DIALOG (Microsoft Support): Print Preview pane + Web Preview pane; "Show page numbers" checkbox (default on); "Right align page numbers" checkbox (default on); "Tab leader" dropdown ((none) / dotted "....." / dashed "----" / solid "____"; default dotted); "Use hyperlinks instead of page numbers" checkbox (web); "Formats" dropdown (From template, Classic, Distinctive, Fancy, Modern, Formal, Simple); "Show levels" spinner (1–9, default 3); plus Options… and Modify… buttons (document those two as not-implemented). OK/Cancel.

ADD TEXT DROPDOWN (References → Table of Contents → Add Text), in order: "Do Not Show in Table of Contents", "Level 1", "Level 2", "Level 3". Sets the OUTLINE/TOC level of the current paragraph: Level 1/2/3 maps it to TOC level 1/2/3; "Do Not Show" removes it from the TOC. Confirmed by Microsoft 365 blog and bettersolutions.

UPDATE TABLE button (and right-click TOC → Update Field): opens "Update Table of Contents" dialog with two radios "Update page numbers only" (refreshes page numbers, keeps entry text) and "Update entire table" (rebuilds entries from current headings), OK/Cancel. Confirmed by bettersolutions and ablebits.

Default automatic TOC includes Heading 1, 2, 3 (3 levels); page numbers right-aligned with a dotted tab leader.
specs.webFindings:
Researched via WebSearch + WebFetch. Sources:
- Microsoft 365 blog "Add text to a table of contents" (microsoft.com) — Add Text → Do Not Show / Level 1/2/3 sets the paragraph's TOC level.
- Microsoft Support "Insert a table of contents" (support.microsoft.com/.../882e8564) — Automatic vs Manual; Manual uses placeholder text you type; Update via right-click → Update Field.
- Microsoft Support "Format or customize a table of contents" (.../9d85eb9c) — Custom dialog: show/align page numbers, tab leader, Formats, Show levels, Modify button.
- bettersolutions.com/word/references/table-of-contents.htm and /ribbon/references-tab.htm — full gallery list (Automatic Table 1/2, Manual Table, Custom…, Remove…, Save Selection…); Add Text dropdown (Do Not Show, Level 1/2/3); Update Table dialog (Update page numbers only / Update entire table); default "dotted tab leader and right aligned page numbers".
- CustomGuide and nutsandboltsspeedtraining.com — the ONLY difference between Automatic Table 1 and 2 is the title text.

CODEBASE FINDING (important): /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/references-tools.js ALREADY EXISTS as an untracked orphan file. It defines a WC.Ref module with insertTOC(manual), updateTOC(), addText(level), plus footnotes/captions/index/citations. BUT it is NOT loaded by index.html (not in the script list, lines 37–57), NOT wired into the commands.js dispatcher, and there is NO CSS for its .wc-toc/.wc-toc-row/.wc-toc-link/.wc-toc-pg/.wc-toc-leader/.wc-toc-title classes. So today the ribbon buttons (cmd: tableOfContents / addText / updateTable in ribbon-data.js lines 1593–1631) fall through to WC.notImplemented. The existing file is a useful skeleton but incomplete: it binds click handlers via addEventListener (won't survive save/load), lacks the gallery menu, Custom/Manual/Remove, and the Update dialog, and addText only re-styles as a Heading rather than setting an independent TOC level.

2. Automatic Table 2: identical but title "Table of Contents".
3. Click a TOC entry → jumps to that heading; test immediately AND after Save → reopen (confirms href-anchor navigation survives load).

6. Manual Table: inserts editable placeholders ("Type chapter title (level n)"); typing over them works; Update Table shows the "manual tables aren't updated" toast (or leaves it).
7. Custom dialog: toggle "Show page numbers" off → numbers gone; "Right align" off → numbers left-adjacent; Tab leader dashed/none → leader changes; Show levels 1 → only Heading 1 entries; OK rebuilds.
8. Remove Table of Contents removes the block cleanly.
9. Save, reopen: TOC persists with correct structure, links still jump, Update Table still works (heading ids preserved).
10. Regression: pagination guides, word count, other ribbon commands still work; getHTML() includes the .wc-toc block and no .pagebreak-guide.

Automated: if the repo has a headless DOM/unit harness, unit-test WC.Ref.headings() filtering (excludes nodes inside .wc-toc, includes data-toc-level paragraphs, excludes level 0) and the page-number recompute path. No existing test runner was confirmed during research.
specs.feasibility: Highly feasible — fully buildable as REAL working behavior, no cloud dependency. Page numbers are real (WC.Editor.pageOfElement returns the visual page of any element and is already used identically by the cross-reference feature). Navigation is real via href="#id" anchors (same mechanism cross-reference uses). Generation from h1/h2/h3 is a straightforward DOM query. A partial skeleton already exists at /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/references-tools.js (WC.Ref) but is orphaned — not loaded in index.html, not wired into commands.js, no CSS — so it currently does nothing. Main work: load + dispatch-wire it; finish the gallery/Custom/Manual/Remove/Update-dialog UI; switch entry links to href anchors (survive save/load); implement Add Text via an independent TOC level; add CSS for .wc-toc. The only genuinely cloud/not-implementable pieces are "More Tables from Office.com" and "Save Selection to Table of Contents Gallery…" (no building-block store) — document those with WC.notImplemented, consistent with the codebase's convention. Key gotchas: recompute page numbers AFTER repaginate(), and rely on href anchors (not JS listeners) for click-to-jump persistence.

specs.feature:
Footnotes & Endnotes — References > Footnotes group. Five controls already exist in ribbon-data.js (lines 1634-1678) with correct cmd ids and tooltips but NO handlers and NO keybindings yet:
- Insert Footnote (cmd: insertFootnote, shortcut Alt+Ctrl+F) — auto-numbered superscript reference mark at the caret + a matching note in a footnotes area at the bottom of the page.
- Insert Endnote (cmd: insertEndnote, shortcut Alt+Ctrl+D) — same mark, note placed in an endnotes area at the end of the document.
- Next Footnote (cmd: nextFootnote, type "split" with items Next Footnote / Previous Footnote / Next Endnote / Previous Endnote) — caret navigation between reference marks.
- Show Notes (cmd: showNotes) — scrolls to / reveals the notes area; if both footnotes and endnotes exist, asks which to view.
- Dialog launcher: the "Footnote & Endnote" dialog (numbering format, location, start at, numbering mode) — wired via the group settings launcher.
Build REAL working behavior: superscript markers with automatic renumbering on insert/delete, a per-page footnotes area + an end-of-document endnotes area, and navigation.
specs.wordBehavior:
Exact Microsoft Word behavior to replicate:

INSERT FOOTNOTE (References > Insert Footnote, Alt+Ctrl+F): inserts a superscript reference mark (default number 1,2,3) at the caret in the body, draws a short separator line above the footnotes area at the bottom of the CURRENT page, inserts the matching superscript number in that area, and drops the caret right after it so you immediately type the note text. Double-clicking the mark in the note jumps back to the reference in the body (and vice-versa).

INSERT ENDNOTE (Insert Endnote, Alt+Ctrl+D): identical, but the note goes to an endnotes area at the END OF THE DOCUMENT, and the default number format is lowercase roman (i, ii, iii). A separator line precedes the endnotes area.

AUTO-NUMBERING: footnotes and endnotes are two independent continuous sequences. Marks renumber automatically in document order whenever you insert or delete a note. You delete a note by deleting its REFERENCE MARK in the body (not the note text); Word then removes the note and renumbers the rest.

NEXT/PREVIOUS FOOTNOTE: split button. Main click = Next Footnote; dropdown gives Next Footnote, Previous Footnote, Next Endnote, Previous Endnote. Moves the caret to the next/previous reference mark of that kind relative to the current caret position and scrolls it into view.

SHOW NOTES: scrolls to the footnote/endnote area for the current note; if the document has both footnotes AND endnotes, Word pops a small "View Footnote area / View Endnote area" chooser first.

FOOTNOTE & ENDNOTE DIALOG (group launcher): a "Location" section with two radios — Footnotes [Bottom of page | Below text] and Endnotes [End of document | End of section]; a "Format" section — Number format dropdown, Custom mark + Symbol, Start at, Numbering [Continuous | Restart each section | Restart each page]; an "Apply changes to" dropdown [Whole document | This section]; and Insert / Cancel / Apply (plus a Convert button to swap footnotes<->endnotes). Number-format choices: "1, 2, 3"; "a, b, c"; "A, B, C"; "i, ii, iii"; "I, II, III"; and the symbol sequence "*, †, ‡, §, **, ††, ..." Default footnote format = 1,2,3 continuous; default endnote format = i,ii,iii continuous.
specs.webFindings:
Confirmed from Microsoft Support and reference sites:
- Shortcuts: Insert Footnote = Alt+Ctrl+F; Insert Endnote = Alt+Ctrl+D (these already match the shortcut strings already present in ribbon-data.js, no contradiction).
- Footnote appears at the bottom of the current page; endnote at the end of the document; caret is placed at the note so you can type immediately. (support.microsoft.com/.../insert-footnotes-and-endnotes-...61f3fb1a)
- Dialog: Number format choices (numbers / letters / roman / symbols), Custom mark/Symbol, Start at, Numbering = Continuous / Restart each section / Restart each page; Location = Footnotes Bottom of page or Below text, Endnotes End of section or End of document; Apply changes to = Whole document or This section; footnote layout supports 1-4 columns. (bettersolutions.com/word/layout/footnotes.htm; geeksforgeeks footnote/endnote guide)
- Deleting the in-body reference mark deletes the note and triggers renumbering of both the body marks and the notes. (support.microsoft.com/.../delete-a-footnote-endnote-or-separator)
- Show Notes pops a chooser when both note types exist. (extendoffice footnote/endnote guide)
Sources: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/insert-footnotes-and-endnotes-in-word-61f3fb1a-4717-414c-9a8f-015a5f3ff4cb ; https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/delete-a-footnote-endnote-or-separator-4183cd36-a421-4dba-aa4c-fa511bba4b3f ; https://bettersolutions.com/word/layout/footnotes.htm ; https://www.ddmcomputing.com/word/keys/wdsk11.htm ; https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/ms-word/how-to-add-footnote-endnote-microsoft-word/

specs.implementationPlan:
Create a new module src/renderer/js/footnotes.js (WC.Notes), mirroring the module pattern of insert-features.js / header-footer.js (IIFE, WC namespace, E = () => WC.Editor). Load it from the renderer HTML alongside the other js files. DATA MODEL — derive numbering from DOM order (no separate state), like wc-field page numbers: - Body marks: N. ID = unique string (Date.now()+counter). - Notes live in
/
, each note =
N editable text
. WC.Notes API: - settings = { fnFormat:'decimal', enFormat:'lower-roman', fnStart:1, enStart:1, fnNumbering:'continuous', enNumbering:'continuous', fnLocation:'bottom', enLocation:'end-doc' } (module-level, persisted in saved HTML via a data-attr on the editor root or a hidden note block — simplest: store on the area div datasets). - ensureArea(kind): create the wc-footnotes/wc-endnotes div if missing. Footnotes area appended before endnotes area (so endnotes stay last). Reuse the contenteditable+separator approach from ensureFooter (header-footer.js:17-21). - insert(kind): E().focus(); E().restoreRange(); generate ID; insertHTML the mark + '​' so caret lands after it; ensureArea(kind); append a wc-note row; renumber(); move caret into the new note's text span (use a Range like caretInto in editor.js:185). Toast minimal or none (Word is silent). - renumber(): for each kind, walk body marks in document order via querySelectorAll('.wc-noteref[data-note="fn"]') (querySelectorAll returns document order), assign n = start-1 + index+1, format with fmt() (reuse/clone fmt+roman from header-footer.js:83-90, add lower/upper-alpha already there, plus a symbolSeq() for *,†,‡,§ repeating-doubling). Then for each note row, look up its mark's number by matching data-note-id and set both the body and the note text. Orphan notes (note whose body mark was deleted) get removed here -> this delivers Word's "delete the mark to delete the note + renumber" behavior. - onInput hook: call WC.Notes.renumber() from Editor's onChange (editor.js:26) — add `if (window.WC.Notes) WC.Notes.renumber();` next to the existing HeaderFooter.refresh()/Insert.refreshFields() calls in repaginate() (editor.js:297-298) OR in the input handler. This makes deletion-driven renumbering automatic. Guard against infinite loops (renumber only mutates text content / removes orphans; it must not call repaginate recursively — set text via textContent only). - next(kind, dir): collect marks of kind in order; find first whose position relative to current caret (compareDocumentPosition / getBoundingClientRect) is after (dir>0) or before; move caret to it and scrollIntoView({block:'center'}). - jump(id): clicking a body mark scrolls to its note; clicking a note back-mark scrolls to the body mark. Wire via a delegated click listener on E().node for .wc-noteref / .wc-noteref-back (add in Notes.init()). - showNotes(): if both areas exist -> flyout chooser; else scrollIntoView the one that exists; if none -> WC.toast('There are no footnotes or endnotes in this document.'). - dialog(): WC.dialog with the Location/Format/Numbering/Apply-to controls; OK/Insert applies settings to module state + renumber() + optionally inserts a note (Insert button) ; Convert swaps data-note fn<->en on all marks/notes and moves note rows between areas, then renumber(). WIRING: 1. commands.js — add handlers near the References block: H.insertFootnote = () => WC.Notes.insert('fn'); H.insertEndnote = () => WC.Notes.insert('en'); H.showNotes = () => WC.Notes.showNotes(); H.nextFootnote = (c,node) => WC.Notes.next('fn',1); // main split click And in Commands.dropdown add: if (cmd==='nextFootnote') return WC.Notes.nextMenu(node); (builds the 4-item flyout calling next('fn',1)/next('fn',-1)/next('en',1)/next('en',-1)). 2. commands.js group-launcher map (around line 448-454, the groupId->dialog map): add footnotes: () => WC.Notes.dialog() so the References > Footnotes dialog launcher opens it. 3. app.js bindKeys() — add: if (e.altKey && k==='f') return () => WC.Notes.insert('fn'); and if (e.altKey && k==='d') return () => WC.Notes.insert('en'); (note: existing handler has `if (k==='d' && !shift) -> Font dialog` and `if (k==='f') -> Find` but BOTH are gated by `if(!mod) return` then matched without altKey; must add the altKey branches BEFORE those non-alt branches, mirroring the existing `e.altKey && k==='1'` heading pattern, and they only fire when e.altKey is true so they won't shadow Ctrl+F/Ctrl+D). e.preventDefault already handled by the dispatcher. 4. icons.js — add a showNotes icon (or alias to insertFootnote like line 130-131). 5. editor.css — add styles for .wc-noteref (sup, blue, cursor pointer), .wc-noteref-back, .wc-footnotes/.wc-endnotes (border-top separator + small "Footnotes"/"Endnotes" ::before label, margin-top, font-size ~10pt), .wc-note (line-height, margin). 6. editor.js getHTML() — the areas are real content and should round-trip (do NOT strip them); ensure renumber() runs on setHTML so loaded docs renumber. Add `if (window.WC.Notes) WC.Notes.renumber();` to setHTML (editor.js:371-379). The areas carry their settings in datasets so they restore. KEY REUSE: fmt()/roman() from header-footer.js:83-90 (alpha+roman already implemented), the ensureFooter pattern, caretInto from editor.js:185, the formatDialog select-options shape from header-footer.js:70.

specs.feature: Citations & Bibliography (References tab → Citations & Bibliography group). Four controls already defined in ribbon-data.js (lines 1702-1759) but unwired: Insert Citation (dropdown), Manage Sources (button), Style (dropdown), Bibliography (dropdown). Build a real, persistent sources store, in-text citation insertion that re-renders per the active style, the Source Manager (master/current lists with Copy/New/Edit/Delete), the Style picker, and a generated formatted bibliography. All implemented as a new WC.References module following the WC.Insert pattern, wired through commands.js.
specs.wordBehavior:
EXACT MS WORD BEHAVIOR (References tab → Citations & Bibliography group):

INSERT CITATION (dropdown):
- Top section lists every source in the document's Current List (each row shows "Author, Title" — clicking inserts an in-text citation field at the caret for that source).
- "Add New Source..." → opens the Create Source dialog (see below), adds it to both Master and Current lists, and inserts a citation at the caret.
- "Add New Placeholder..." → prompts for a tag name (no spaces); inserts a placeholder citation shown as "(Placeholder1)" / the tag in the text. Placeholders appear in Source Manager with a "?" (question mark) marker and can be edited later into real sources.
- An inserted in-text citation is a clickable field; clicking its dropdown gives Edit Citation (add page/suppress author/year/title), Edit Source, Convert citation to static text, Update Citations and Bibliography.

CREATE SOURCE dialog (also reached via Source Manager → New/Edit):
- "Type of Source" dropdown: Book, Book Section, Journal Article, Article in a Periodical, Conference Proceedings, Report, Web site, Document From Web site, Electronic Source, Art, Sound Recording, Performance, Film, Interview, Patent, Case, Miscellaneous.
- Fields shown depend on Type. Required fields marked with a red asterisk. Common bibliography fields: Author, Title, Year, City, Publisher, Editor, Journal/Periodical Title, Volume, Issue, Pages, Edition, Month, Day, URL, DOI, Medium, Translator, Producer, Conference Name, Standard Number.
- "Show All Bibliography Fields" checkbox reveals the full (optional) field set.
- Author field has a separate "Edit" entry helper (Last/First/Middle, add multiple, Corporate Author toggle).
- "Tag name" auto-generated at bottom (e.g. first author surname + year, like "Smi21").

MANAGE SOURCES → Source Manager dialog:
- Two list panes: Master List (left, persisted across all documents) and Current List (right, sources in this document).
- Search box (filters by author/title/year/tag); "Sort by" dropdown (Author / Title / Tag / Year).
- Buttons between/under the lists: Copy -> (copy selected from Master to Current, or Current to Master — arrow flips by focus), Delete, Edit, New.
- Markers in list rows: checkmark = source cited in current document; question mark = placeholder (incomplete source).
- Preview pane at bottom shows the in-text citation and bibliography entry rendered in the current Style.
- Close button.

STYLE (dropdown): Picks the active citation style. The Word default 12-style list (alphabetical-ish as shown in Word): APA (Seventh Edition), Chicago (Seventeenth Edition), GB7714, GOST - Name Sort, GOST - Title Sort, Harvard - Anglia, IEEE, ISO 690 - First Element and Date, ISO 690 - Numerical Reference, MLA (Ninth Edition), SIST02, Turabian. Selecting a style immediately reformats all in-text citations AND any inserted bibliography. Author-date styles (APA/MLA/Chicago/Harvard/GOST/ISO-date/Turabian) render in-text as (Author Year) or (Author Page); numeric styles (IEEE, ISO 690 - Numerical, SIST02) render in-text as [1], [2]... numbered in citation order.

BIBLIOGRAPHY (dropdown): Built-in gallery blocks plus a command:
- "Bibliography" (heading "Bibliography"), "References" (heading "References"), "Works Cited" (heading "Works Cited") — each a content-control block with the chosen heading + the formatted source list.
- "Insert Bibliography" — inserts the list with NO heading at the caret.
- "Save Selection to Bibliography Gallery..." (not implemented; toast).
- The inserted bibliography is a live field: choosing a different Style or adding sources + "Update Citations and Bibliography" re-renders it. Entries are alphabetized by author for author-date styles, or in citation order (numbered) for IEEE/numeric styles. Hanging indent applied.

IN-TEXT FORMATS (must match):
- APA 7: paraphrase (Author, Year); quote (Author, Year, p. 14); no author -> (Title, Year); no date -> n.d.
- MLA 9: (Author Page) e.g. (Smith 8); no page -> (Smith).
- Chicago (author-date) / Harvard: (Author Year, page).
- IEEE / numeric: [1] in square brackets, numbered in order of first appearance.

BIBLIOGRAPHY ENTRY FORMATS (must match):
- APA 7 book: Author, A. A. (Year). *Title in sentence case*. Publisher. (italic title, hanging indent, alphabetized)
- APA 7 journal: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of article. *Journal Title*, *Vol*(Issue), pp-pp. (article title roman, journal italic+title case, volume italic)
- MLA 9 book: Author Last, First. *Title*. Publisher, Year.
- MLA 9 journal: Author Last, First. \"Title of Article.\" *Journal Title*, vol. #, no. #, Year, pp. ##-##. (article in quotes, journal italic)
- IEEE journal: [n] A. B. Author, \"Title of article,\" *Journal Abbrev.*, vol. #, no. #, pp. ##-##, Year. (initials before surname, article in quotes, journal italic, numbered)
- Chicago/Turabian/Harvard handled as author-date variants of the above.
specs.webFindings:
Microsoft default 12-style Style dropdown (confirmed via Microsoft Support + ATU/KU library guides): APA, Chicago, GB7714, GOST - Name Sort, GOST - Title Sort, Harvard - Anglia, IEEE, ISO 690 - First Element and Date, ISO 690 - Numerical Reference, MLA, SIST02, Turabian. Word notes the styles are "set by Microsoft"; current builds label APA as Seventh Edition, MLA as Ninth Edition, Chicago Seventeenth. Additional downloadable styles (Vancouver, AMA, ACS, Nature, etc.) come from BibWord but are NOT built in.

Source Manager (Microsoft Support / Office-Watch): opened from References > Citations & Bibliography > Manage Sources. Has Master List (saved separately, reusable across docs) and Current List (saved in the document). Copy moves a selected source between lists; New/Edit open the Create Source dialog; Delete removes from a list. Search box + Sort by (Author/Title/Tag/Year). Checkmark = cited; question mark = placeholder. Preview pane shows rendered citation + bibliography.

Create Source dialog: "Type of Source" dropdown changes the visible fields; required fields have red asterisks; "Show All Bibliography Fields" reveals the full set; tag name auto-generated.

Citation format rules verified from APA Style / library guides:
- APA 7 in-text: (Author, Year) for paraphrase; add p. # for quotes; n.d. when no date. Reference list alphabetized, hanging indent. Book: Author, A. A. (Year). *Title sentence case*. Publisher. Journal: Author, A. A. (Year). Article title sentence case. *Journal Title*, *Vol*(Issue), pp.
- MLA 9 in-text: author-page (Smith 8); name only if no page. Works Cited core elements (author, title, container, ...): Book: Author Last, First. *Title*. Publisher, Year. Article: Author Last, First. \"Title.\" *Journal*, vol. #, no. #, Year, pp. ##.
- IEEE in-text: bracketed number [1], numbered in citation order, reused for repeats. Reference list numeric in citation order; initials before surname; article title in quotes; journal italic+abbreviated; vol./no./pp./year.

specs.implementationPlan:
Create ONE new file: /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/references.js (IIFE exporting WC.References), mirroring insert-features.js structure. Load it via the same script list that includes insert-features.js (check index.html / app bootstrap and add a <script> tag). Then wire commands in commands.js.

DATA MODEL & STORE (in references.js):
- A source object: { id, tag, type, placeholder:false, authors:[{last,first,middle,corporate}], title, year, month, day, city, publisher, journal, volume, issue, pages, edition, url, doi, medium, editor, translator, conference, standardNumber }.
- WC.References.master = [] (Master List), WC.References.current = [] (Current List for the doc). Persist master in localStorage key 'wc-citation-master'; load on init. Current list is derived from the document (serialized into the doc via a hidden JSON block) OR kept on the editor and re-saved with the file. Simplest faithful approach: keep current[] in memory + mirror into a hidden <script type="application/json" id="wc-sources"> inside #editor so it round-trips through getHTML/save (Files sanitize must allow it — verify; if stripped, store in WC.Files doc metadata instead). Default to in-memory + localStorage 'wc-citation-current-<docname>' as a pragmatic fallback.
- WC.References.style = localStorage 'wc-citation-style' || 'APA'.

STYLE ENGINE (pure functions, the core deliverable):
- STYLES = { 'APA': {label:'APA (Seventh Edition)', numeric:false, inText:fn, biblio:fn, sortByAuthor:true, heading:'References'}, 'MLA': {... heading:'Works Cited'}, 'Chicago':{...}, 'Harvard - Anglia':{...}, 'IEEE':{numeric:true, heading:'References'}, 'ISO 690 - Numerical Reference':{numeric:true}, 'ISO 690 - First Element and Date':{...}, 'Turabian':{...}, 'GOST - Name Sort':{...}, 'GOST - Title Sort':{...}, 'GB7714':{numeric:true}, 'SIST02':{numeric:true} }. Implement APA/MLA/IEEE/Chicago/Harvard fully; map the GOST/ISO/SIST02/GB7714/Turabian variants onto the closest implemented formatter (author-date -> APA-like, numeric -> IEEE-like) so every built-in style produces real output (document the approximation in a code comment + tooltip), matching the "build real behavior, document gaps" directive.
- formatInText(source, style, {page}) -> string for the <span>. Author-date: surname + ', ' + year (+ ', p. N' if page) wrapped in parens; placeholder -> '(' + tag + ')'; numeric -> '[' + number + ']' where number = index in citation-order list.
- formatBiblio(source, style) -> HTML string (with <i> for italics) for one entry.
- helper: authorList(source, style) handles "Last, F. M." vs "F. M. Last" (IEEE) vs "Last, First" (MLA) and multiple-author joining (& / and / commas / et al. by style), surname-for-sort.
- citationOrder(): array of source ids in order of first <span.wc-citation> appearance in the document (querySelectorAll), used to assign numeric labels.

RENDER / REFRESH:
- WC.References.refresh(): (1) recompute numeric order; (2) for every span.wc-citation in #editor, re-render textContent from its data-cite-id + data-page using the active style; (3) for every .wc-bibliography, rebuild its entries from the current source set (sorted per style). Call after style change, source add/edit/delete, and on document load. Mark E().dirty, E().repaginate().

INSERT FLOWS:
- insertCitation(source, {page}): E().focus(); E().restoreRange(); build span; E().insertHTML(span + ' ') (pattern from Insert.insertField). If source not in current[], add it.
- insertBibliography(headingLabel|null): build <div class="wc-bibliography"> and insertHTML; if headingLabel, prepend heading paragraph.

DIALOGS: createSourceDialog(existing?, onSave), sourceManagerDialog(), per the UI section. Field schema per type stored in a TYPE_FIELDS map ({Book:['author*','title*','year*','city','publisher*'], 'Journal Article':['author*','title*','journal*','year*','volume','issue','pages'], 'Web site':['author','title*','year','url*','accessed'], ...}). Tag auto-gen: firstAuthorSurname.slice(0,3) + year.slice(-2), de-duplicated with a letter suffix.

specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible — this is a strong fit for the clone's architecture and one of the most genuinely implementable References features (it's pure text/DOM, no cloud dependency, unlike Researcher/Smart Lookup which are correctly marked feasible:"no"). Everything needed already exists: the WC.Insert module pattern to copy, WC.dialog/flyout/flyItem/flyHeader for all UI, E().insertHTML + saveRange/restoreRange for insertion (same pattern as Insert.insertField / bookmarks / cross-reference), E().repaginate for flow, and localStorage for the Master List. The four ribbon controls already exist (ribbon-data.js 1702-1759) and just need handlers in commands.js (H.insertCitation/manageSources/style/bibliography + Commands.dropdown cases), exactly mirroring how Insert-tab dropdowns are wired today.

CORE WORK is the style engine (pure formatter functions) — moderate but bounded; APA/MLA/IEEE/Chicago/Harvard are well-specified above and the remaining Word built-ins (GOST/ISO/SIST02/GB7714/Turabian) are faithfully approximated by routing to the nearest author-date or numeric formatter with a documented comment, satisfying "build real behavior, document genuine gaps."

RISKS / WATCH-OUTS (all manageable):
1. Persistence round-trip: confirm WC.Files.sanitize() does not strip data-cite-id / class="wc-citation" / the hidden sources JSON. If it does, either add to its allowlist or store current-list JSON in document metadata. Master List via localStorage is safe.
2. cmd name 'style' is generic — verified no existing handler/dropdown collision today, but keep it scoped to the References dropdown wiring.
3. Numeric (IEEE) ordering must be recomputed from document order on every refresh, and the bibliography re-sorted accordingly; covered by citationOrder() + refresh().
4. Edit Citation (page #, suppress author) and Save-to-Gallery are the only non-essential bits; Save-to-Gallery is a toast/notImplemented like sibling controls, and Edit Citation can be phase 2 without hurting the core flow.

Four controls, already present in ribbon-data.js (References tab, "Captions" group, ids references.captions.*): Insert Caption (cmd insertCaption), Insert Table of Figures (cmd insertTableOfFigures), Update Table (cmd updateTable), Cross-reference (cmd crossReference — ALREADY IMPLEMENTED as WC.Insert.crossRefDialog). specs.wordBehavior:
INSERT CAPTION (real Word): A caption is a numbered label paragraph attached to an object. Dialog fields: (1) Caption text box showing the live label+number prefix e.g. "Figure 1" that the user appends descriptive text to; (2) Label dropdown — built-in list Figure / Table / Equation, plus "New Label..." (type a custom label name) and "Delete Label"; (3) Position dropdown — "Above selected item" / "Below selected item" (DEFAULT: Figure=Below, Table=Above, Equation=Below); (4) "Exclude label from caption" checkbox (shows just the number); (5) Numbering... button -> Caption Numbering dialog with Format dropdown (1,2,3 / a,b,c / A,B,C / i,ii,iii / I,II,III), plus "Include chapter number" checkbox + "Chapter starts with style" (Heading 1/2/3) + "Use separator" (hyphen/period/colon/em-dash) producing e.g. "Figure 2-1". Each LABEL has its own independent running sequence counter, numbered by document order. Caption format is: Label + single space + number (+ optional separator + typed text), e.g. "Figure 1" or "Figure 1: My chart". The caption paragraph uses the "Caption" paragraph style (created on first caption: ~9pt, often italic/colored, spacing). Caption is inserted as a NEW paragraph directly above or below the selected object/paragraph. Renumbering: inserting a caption mid-document renumbers all later captions of that label.

INSERT TABLE OF FIGURES: Collects all captions of ONE selected label into a list sorted by page number (document order), each line = caption text on the left, page number on the right. Dialog options: Caption label dropdown (Figure/Table/Equation/custom); "Show page numbers" checkbox (default on); "Right align page numbers" checkbox (default on); "Tab leader" dropdown (none / dots (default) / dashes / underline) — only when right-aligned; "Include label and number" checkbox (default on; if off, shows only the descriptive caption text). Page numbers right-aligned with dotted tab leader by default.

UPDATE TABLE: Right-click a TOF (or References > Update Table) opens a small chooser: "Update page numbers only" vs "Update entire table" (rebuild list from current captions). This is the SHARED updateTable command (also used by TOC and Table of Authorities), so the handler must detect which field block the caret/selection is in or update all TOF blocks.
specs.webFindings:
Confirmed via Microsoft Support and guides:
- Default labels: Equation, Figure, Table; New Label adds custom (support.microsoft.com/.../82fa82a4...).
- Position default: Figure=Below selected item, Table=Above selected item.
- Numbering formats: arabic numerals, letters, roman numerals; per-label sequence; optional chapter numbering needs unique Heading style + separator -> "Figure 2-1" (support.microsoft.com/.../76aa8706...). Underlying field is {STYLEREF n \s}{SEQ Figure \* ARABIC \s n}.
- Caption format = Label + space + number; the number/text separator is a symbol, not a space; a "Caption" style is created on first caption.
- Table of Figures (support.microsoft.com/.../c5ea59c5...): References > Insert Table of Figures; searches doc for captions, lists sorted by page; options Show page numbers, Right align page numbers, Tab leader (dots default), Caption label, Include label and number.
- Update Table: "Update page numbers only" or "Update entire table"; also via right-click Update Field.
Sources: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/add-format-or-delete-captions-in-word-82fa82a4-f0f3-438f-a422-34bb5cef9c81 ; https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/insert-a-table-of-figures-c5ea59c5-487c-4fb2-bd48-e34dd57f0ec1 ; https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/add-chapter-numbers-to-captions-in-word-76aa8706-3453-4a72-ad5c-b87d90ce1698

specs.testPlan:
Manual (electron app) since codebase has no unit harness for renderer DOM:
1. Insert an image, select it, References > Insert Caption -> choose Figure, Below -> verify a "Figure 1" caption paragraph appears below the image; type "Sales chart" -> "Figure 1 Sales chart".
2. Insert a second figure + caption -> "Figure 2"; insert a Table caption -> "Table 1" (independent counters).
3. Insert a caption BEFORE Figure 1 in document order -> verify later figures renumber (now Figure 2, Figure 3) via renumberCaptions.
4. Change Numbering to A,B,C and to i,ii,iii -> verify format applied per label.
5. Enable Include chapter number with Heading 1 + hyphen under a "Heading 1" -> caption reads "Figure 1-1".
6. References > Insert Table of Figures (label=Figure) -> verify list of all figure captions with right-aligned page numbers and dotted leader; toggle Show page numbers / Right align / Tab leader and re-insert to confirm options.
7. Add content to push a figure to page 2, then Update Table > Update page numbers only -> page column updates (also verify it auto-updates live because refreshFields runs on repaginate). Update entire table after adding a new caption -> new row appears.
8. Save (getHTML) and reopen (setHTML) -> captions and TOF persist; numbers/pages recompute correctly.
9. Cross-reference (already done) still works against captions (optional: confirm headings/bookmarks unaffected).
Edge cases: no object selected (insert caption at caret on its own line); empty label list after deleting custom label; TOF with zero captions of that label shows "No table of figures entries found." placeholder (Word behavior).
specs.feasibility: Fully feasible as REAL working behavior — no cloud dependency. Everything needed already exists: WC.Editor.pageOfElement(el) (editor.js:320) for page numbers, the refreshFields hook already called inside repaginate() (editor.js:298, insert-features.js:244) for live updates, WC.dialog/el/flyout/escapeHtml/toast helpers, the ribbon controls already wired to cmds insertCaption/insertTableOfFigures/updateTable, and a proven sibling implementation (crossRefDialog) to copy. Caption numbering, per-label sequencing, chapter numbering, and the Table of Figures with page numbers are all plain DOM + the existing pagination API. The only Word features intentionally approximated (not blockers): true STYLEREF/SEQ field codes are simulated with data-* attributes + a renumber pass (visually identical); the "Caption" style is a class rather than an editable named style entry in the style gallery. Estimated ~250-320 lines across insert-features.js (dialogs + renumber + TOF build/update) and ~6 lines in commands.js. No changes to ribbon-data.js needed.

specs.feature: References tab — Index group (Mark Entry / Insert Index / Update Index), Table of Authorities group (Mark Citation / Insert Table of Authorities / Update Table), and Research group (Search/Smart Lookup, Researcher). Index and Table of Authorities are built as real working behavior using hidden field-code spans; Researcher and Smart Lookup are genuinely cloud-backed and ship as honest documented stubs.
specs.wordBehavior:
INDEX GROUP (References > Index):
- Mark Entry (Alt+Shift+X): Select text, click Mark Entry -> modal "Mark Index Entry" dialog. Fields: Main entry (prefilled with selection); Subentry (a 2nd level; 3rd level via "sub:subsub" colon syntax). Options radio: Cross-reference (textbox prefilled "See "), Current page [default], Page range (with a Bookmark dropdown). Page number format: Bold checkbox, Italic checkbox. Buttons: Mark (this instance) and Mark All (every matching occurrence of the main-entry text). Each mark inserts a hidden XE field after the text. (Real Word's dialog is modeless; a modal re-reading the selection on each open is an acceptable clone simplification.) XE syntax: { XE "Main:Sub" } with \b (bold page no.), \i (italic page no.), \t "text" (text instead of page, used for cross-refs), \r (run-in); colon separates main:sub; escaped \: for a literal colon. XE fields are hidden text, visible only when Show/Hide is on.
- Insert Index: "Index" dialog. Type = Indented vs Run-in; Columns (default 2); Right align page numbers (checkbox); Tab leader dropdown (none/dots/dashes/underline, enabled only with right-align); Formats dropdown (From template, Classic, Fancy, Modern, Bulleted, Formal, Simple) with Print Preview. OK builds an alphabetized index from all XE fields: main entries with nested subentries, de-duped+sorted page numbers, cross-refs shown as "See X" with no page number.
- Update Index: F9 or Update Index rebuilds the index in place from current XE fields and recomputes page numbers.

TABLE OF AUTHORITIES GROUP:
- Mark Citation (Alt+Shift+I): Select the long citation -> "Mark Citation" dialog. Fields: Selected/long-citation text (editable, formatting preserved), Category dropdown (1 Cases, 2 Statutes, 3 Other Authorities, 4 Rules, 5 Treatises, 6 Regulations, 7 Constitutional Provisions; slots 8-16 user-customizable via Category... sub-dialog), Short citation (used to auto-detect later cites). Buttons: Mark, Mark All (all matches of the short citation), Next Citation (heuristic jump to text with "v." or section symbol). Inserts hidden TA field: { TA \l "long" \s "short" \c categoryNumber \b }.
- Insert Table of Authorities: dialog with Category list (All [default] + named categories); "Use passim" checkbox (5+ pages -> "passim"); "Keep original formatting" checkbox (carry bold/italic/underline into the TOA); Tab leader dropdown; Formats dropdown (From template, Classic, Distinctive, Formal, Simple) with Print Preview. OK builds the TOA: one section heading per non-empty category, citations alphabetized within category, each followed by sorted/de-duped page numbers (or "passim").
- Update Table: rebuilds the TOA in place from current TA fields.

RESEARCH GROUP:
- Search / Smart Lookup: opens a right "Search"/"Smart Lookup" task pane with Explore (Bing web/images/Wikipedia) and Define (Oxford dictionary) tabs; powered by the Bing Knowledge Graph; cloud-only; also reachable via right-click > Smart Lookup.
- Researcher: opens a research pane (Microsoft 365 subscription only, Bing-powered) returning reputable topics/sources; clicking + adds a heading/source and a cited link/comment. Cloud + subscription-gated.
specs.webFindings:
Microsoft Support confirms: Mark Entry inserts an XE field; Insert Index reads all XE fields into an alphabetical index; Update Index = click index + F9. XE switches verified: \b bold page no., \i italic page no., \t "text" substitutes text for the page number (cross-refs "See ..."), \r run-in subentries; colon separates main:subentry; escaped colon \: for literal colon (e.g. "Luke 9\:21"). Index dialog options verified: Indented vs Run-in, Columns, Right align page numbers, Tab leader, Formats (Classic/Fancy/Modern/Bulleted/Formal/Simple/From template). [support.microsoft.com/.../create-and-update-an-index-cc502c71 ; officemastery.com/word-index-switches ; documentation.help/ms-office-word-2003/worefXEIndexEntry1.htm]

Table of Authorities (Microsoft Support + law-school LibGuides): Mark Citation = Alt+Shift+I, inserts a TA field. Syntax { TA \l "long" \s "short" \c N \b }; \l = long citation appearing in the TOA, \s = short citation (does NOT appear; matches later cites), \c = category number. Default categories: 1 Cases, 2 Statutes, 3 Other Authorities, 4 Rules, 5 Treatises, 6 Regulations, 7 Constitutional Provisions; user slots 8-16. Insert TOA dialog: Category (default All), Use passim (5+ pages -> "passim"), Keep original formatting, Tab leader, Formats with Print Preview. [support.microsoft.com/.../create-a-table-of-authorities-ddd126ae ; legalofficeguru.com/table-of-authorities-ultimate-guide ; learn.microsoft.com/.../word.tableofauthorities.passim]

Researcher / Smart Lookup (Microsoft Support + Microsoft Learn): both are connected/cloud experiences powered by the Bing Knowledge Graph. Smart Lookup pane has Explore (Bing web/images/Wikipedia) and Define (Oxford) tabs. Researcher requires a Microsoft 365 subscription, English/FR/IT/DE/ES/JA only. Neither runs offline -> correctly stubbed. [support.microsoft.com/.../smart-lookup-debf2083 ; support.microsoft.com/.../research-your-paper-easily-within-word ; learn.microsoft.com/.../optional-connected-experiences]

specs.implementationPlan:
All work centers on the existing-but-unwired references-tools.js plus three small wiring edits and CSS.

STEP 0 — Load + wire (the whole module is currently dead):
- index.html (src/renderer/index.html): add <script src="js/references-tools.js"></script> BEFORE commands.js (so WC.Ref exists when handlers register).
- commands.js: add handlers in the H map near H.crossReference (line 98): H.markEntry, H.insertIndex, H.updateIndex, H.markCitation, H.insertTableOfAuthorities, H.search, H.researcher, delegating to WC.Ref.* . Also verify/connect the already-present TOC/footnote/caption WC.Ref methods (they are likewise unwired). updateTable cmd is shared by Captions and TOA groups -> have H.updateTable call WC.Ref.updateAny() which already branches on which table exists, or route via the group-aware launcher.

STEP 1 — INDEX (extend references-tools.js):
- Replace the visible .wc-index span with a hidden field span: <span class="wc-field wc-xe" data-main data-sub data-xref data-bold data-italic> inserted AFTER the marked text. Store XE switches as data-* for round-trip. Field code shows only under #editor.show-marks.
- markEntryDialog(): build the Mark Index Entry dialog. Mark = one XE field at selection end. Mark All = find every text node in #editor containing the main-entry string and insert an XE field after each (skip inside existing fields / TOC / index blocks).
- insertIndexDialog(): collect all .wc-xe fields into a map keyed by main entry -> { subs:{sub->pages[]}, pages:[], xrefs:[] } using E().pageOfElement(field). localeCompare-sort keys; de-dupe+sort page numbers; render Indented vs Run-in; cross-refs render "See <text>" (no pages); honor bold/italic on page numbers; columns via CSS column-count; right-align + tab-leader via flex row with dotted .wc-leader. Wrap in <div class="wc-index-list" contenteditable="false"> like existing wc-toc.
- updateIndex(): if .wc-index-list exists, regenerate in place using the same builder and options stashed as data-* on the wrapper.

STEP 2 — TABLE OF AUTHORITIES (new methods):
- Categories array (1-7 named + slots 8-16 empty) stored on WC.Ref so Category... can rename them.
- markCitationDialog(): long-citation textarea (from selection), category select, short-citation input. Mark inserts hidden <span class="wc-field wc-ta" data-long data-short data-cat> after the selection. Mark All inserts after each occurrence of the short citation. Next Citation = scan forward for /\bv\.\b|§/ and select it.
- insertTOADialog(): group .wc-ta fields by category; alphabetize by long citation within category; collect+sort pages; Use passim + pageCount>=5 -> "passim"; Keep original formatting -> clone the citation inline HTML rather than text; render category headers + dot leaders. Wrap in <div class="wc-toa" contenteditable="false">.
- updateTOA(): regenerate in place.

STEP 3 — RESEARCH (honest stub):
- smartLookup() and researcher(): open a small right task pane (reuse existing pane styling) with an Explore/Define mock for Smart Lookup and a clear message that these are connected (cloud) experiences powered by the Bing Knowledge Graph requiring a Microsoft account / Microsoft 365 subscription + internet, unavailable offline; link to docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md. (WC.notImplemented toast is the minimum fallback.) Both already feasible:"no" in ribbon-data.js.

STEP 4 — CSS (src/renderer/styles/editor.css, near .find-hit ~line 209):
- .wc-field { display:none; } and #editor.show-marks .wc-field { display:inline; color:#7a7a7a; background:#e8eef7; font-size:.85em; } with Word-style gray field shading and the { XE }/{ TA } code shown from data-*.
- .wc-index-list { column-count:N; } category-header bold for .wc-toa; .wc-leader dotted fill, right-aligned page numbers reusing the existing .wc-toc-leader/.wc-toc-pg approach.

STEP 5 — Shortcuts (app.js bindKeys, lines 47-86):
- Current handler does `if(!mod) return;` (mod = Ctrl/Cmd) BEFORE checking alt, so Alt+Shift+X/I never fire. Add a branch ABOVE that guard: if (e.altKey && e.shiftKey && k==='x') { e.preventDefault(); WC.Ref.markEntryDialog(); return; } and the same for k==='i' -> WC.Ref.markCitationDialog().

specs.feasibility: Highly feasible and largely real. Index and Table of Authorities are fully buildable offline as genuine working features: hidden field spans (XE/TA) carrying data-* switches, DOM aggregation off the existing E().pageOfElement() pagination, sorted/de-duped page lists, in-place rebuild — mirroring the already-shipped TOC/Table-of-Figures code in the same file. Page numbers are as accurate as the clone's pagination model (visual/CSS-based), the same basis the existing TOC and cross-reference features use, so fidelity is consistent with the rest of the app. ~90% of Word's observable behavior is replicable: dialogs, Mark/Mark All, subentries, cross-references, categories, passim, Keep original formatting, leaders, Update. Honest gaps: (1) real OOXML round-trip of XE/TA field codes to .docx (the app uses html-to-docx, lower fidelity — fields persist in the app's own HTML save but may not export as true Word fields); (2) Word's exact alphabetization/letter-heading nuances and Print Preview thumbnails are approximated. Researcher and Smart Lookup are genuinely cloud/subscription features (Bing Knowledge Graph, Microsoft 365) and cannot run offline — correctly shipped as an honest documented stub, consistent with their existing feasible:"no" marking in ribbon-data.js. Net: build Index + Table of Authorities for real; stub Research with a clear, documented explanation.
